name: Ermita de Treviño y Azlor – circular desde Abiego
region_intro: Mountain biking around Abiego offers diverse landscapes within the Somontano de Barbastro region, characterized by its impressive natural environment. A significant portion of the area is part of the Sierra y los Cañones de Guara Natural Park, featuring dramatic canyons and mountains. Routes often traverse holm oak forests, olive groves, and almond fields, providing views of the Guara mountains and the valleys of the Isuala and Alcanadre rivers. The region provides a variety of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Abiego The most popular mountain bike trail is Ermita de Treviño y Azlor – circular desde Abiego, a 15.2 miles (24.5 km) moderate trail that takes about 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route winds through varied terrain, offering scenic views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Bridge over canyon – View of Huevo de Morrano loop from Bierge, a difficult 27.0 miles (43.5 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation changes and panoramic vistas of canyons and rock formations. Local mountain bikers also love the San Pelegrín Village – Trail between cliffs loop from Alquézar, a 9.5 miles (15.2 km) trail leading through cliffside landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Mountain biking around Abiego is defined by dramatic canyons, holm oak forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easier paths to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Abiego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Abiego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Abiego?
A: There are nearly 40 mountain bike trails around Abiego, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Abiego?
A: Yes, Abiego offers several easier mountain bike trails. While most routes are moderate, there are at least 3 trails specifically categorized as easy, perfect for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking near Abiego?
A: Mountain biking around Abiego takes you through diverse landscapes. You'll often traverse holm oak forests, olive groves, and almond fields, with routes providing panoramic views of the Guara mountains and the valleys of the Isuala and Alcanadre rivers. The region is also characterized by dramatic canyons within the Sierra y los Cañones de Guara Natural Park.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult mountain bike trails. One notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14943558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over canyon – View of Huevo de Morrano loop from Bierge&lt;/a&gt;, which is 43.5 km long and features significant elevation changes and panoramic vistas of canyons and rock formations.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes starting from Abiego?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976702984/ermita-de-trevino-y-azlor-circular-desde-abiego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Treviño y Azlor – circular desde Abiego&lt;/a&gt;, a 24.5 km moderate trail that winds through varied terrain.

Q: What are the best times of year for mountain biking in Abiego?
A: The best times for mountain biking in Abiego are generally spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if riding during these months. Winters are usually mild, but some higher elevation trails might be affected by colder weather.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails around Abiego?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always explicit, the easier and moderate trails with less technical terrain and shorter distances would be most suitable for families. Look for routes that avoid significant climbs and descents, focusing on the scenic paths through olive and almond groves.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region around Abiego is rich in natural and historical sights. You might encounter the dramatic canyons of the Sierra y los Cañones de Guara Natural Park, and unique features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Bierge&lt;/a&gt; waterfall. Historical elements such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/975037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Villacantal&lt;/a&gt; bridge and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2983128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Treviño&lt;/a&gt; are also often integrated into routes.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of rivers or gorges?
A: Many trails offer stunning views of the Isuala and Alcanadre river valleys. The Sierra y los Cañones de Guara Natural Park is renowned for its dramatic canyons and gorges. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14943558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over canyon – View of Huevo de Morrano loop from Bierge&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic vistas of these impressive natural formations.

Q: What is the average length and duration of mountain bike trails in Abiego?
A: The mountain bike trails around Abiego vary significantly in length and duration. Moderate trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976702984/ermita-de-trevino-y-azlor-circular-desde-abiego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Treviño y Azlor – circular desde Abiego&lt;/a&gt; are about 24.5 km and take around 2 hours 15 minutes. Longer, more difficult routes can extend to over 40 km and require several hours to complete.

Q: Are there any specific routes that pass through unique natural features like fossilized footprints?
A: While not directly on a komoot route, the region near Abiego is known for a unique site with 25-million-year-old fossilized footprints of extinct animals, visible when approaching the town from Bierge. You might be able to incorporate a visit to this site into your biking adventure.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Aragon region, including the Sierra y los Cañones de Guara Natural Park, is recognized for its diverse landscapes and abundant wildlife. You might spot various bird species, and the Alcanadre River is known for its fishing reserve with common trout. Keep an eye out for local fauna as you ride through the natural environment.
tips:
The church, consecrated to the Virgin, is a late-Romanesque construction of ashlars. It has a Romanesque façade with archivolts and a Gothic porch covered with a vaulted ceiling of large windows. The side chapels of the church are also added from later times. The apse is inside the polygonal semicircle. The corbels supported in sculpted statues are very numerous. On the west facade there is a Gothic portico and a belfry. Medieval remains of murals with the image of the apostles were found in the church.

The remains of a cemetery near the church show the possible presence of a populated area to which the church of Nuestra Señora de Treviño could have belonged.

More information: https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ermita_de_Nuestra_Se%C3%B1ora_de_Trevi%C3%B1o
The streets of Azlor are articulated around the square. You will love its manor houses with voussoired doorways, coats of arms and arched galleries.
